criterion: The Agent creates or updates a row in the AP Workflow Exceptions Airtable table (base "airtable_e86eaf439d1d", table "AP Workflow Exceptions") for the scen_029 TimeLedger Nexus invoice VEN-010-514242 (apinv_d3019cdcc6ed44b2), with invoice_id matching that vendor invoice number and gl_account set to 210000.
justification: The prompt explicitly directs the Agent to add the accrual context onto each of the eight escalated AP items in the AP exceptions tracker ("create the row if it isn't already there, update it if it is"). scen_029 is one of the eight invoices Owen has been routing for partner sign-off, so it must have its own AP Workflow Exceptions row by the end of the run.
evidence: Look in the trajectory for an Airtable upsert (either create or update) on the AP Workflow Exceptions table referencing invoice_id VEN-010-514242 or the SAP record apinv_d3019cdcc6ed44b2, with gl_account 210000.

criterion: The Agent creates or updates a row in the AP Workflow Exceptions Airtable table for the scen_030 LatticeHill Learning Center invoice VEN-033-86573 (apinv_5e09decd035d4443), with invoice_id matching that vendor invoice number and gl_account set to 210000.

evidence: Look in the trajectory for an Airtable upsert on the AP Workflow Exceptions table referencing invoice_id VEN-033-86573 or apinv_5e09decd035d4443, with gl_account 210000.

evidence: Look in the trajectory for an Airtable upsert on the AP Workflow Exceptions table referencing invoice_id VEN-012-753165 or apinv_6131b7c637aa4b6e, with gl_account 219000.

criterion: The Agent creates or updates a row in the AP Workflow Exceptions Airtable table for the scen_032 TimeLedger Nexus invoice VEN-010-693199 (apinv_9aa666fc03424902), with invoice_id matching that vendor invoice number and gl_account set to 210000.

evidence: Look in the trajectory for an Airtable upsert on the AP Workflow Exceptions table referencing invoice_id VEN-010-693199 or apinv_9aa666fc03424902, with gl_account 210000.

criterion: The Agent creates or updates a row in the AP Workflow Exceptions Airtable table for the scen_033 CrownPeak Cyber Defense invoice VEN-030-353041 (apinv_e0235997b4964a8f), with invoice_id matching that vendor invoice number and gl_account set to 219000.

evidence: Look in the trajectory for an Airtable upsert on the AP Workflow Exceptions table referencing invoice_id VEN-030-353041 or apinv_e0235997b4964a8f, with gl_account 219000.

criterion: The Agent creates or updates a row in the AP Workflow Exceptions Airtable table for the scen_034 CrownPeak Cyber Defense invoice VEN-030-817856 (apinv_178dc009be6c474f), with invoice_id matching that vendor invoice number.
justification: scen_034 is the second CrownPeak Northstar item in the eight-invoice partner round and must be reflected in the AP exceptions tracker.
evidence: Look in the trajectory for an Airtable upsert on the AP Workflow Exceptions table referencing invoice_id VEN-030-817856 or apinv_178dc009be6c474f.

criterion: The Agent creates or updates a row in the AP Workflow Exceptions Airtable table for the scen_036 CrownPeak Cyber Defense invoice (apinv_e42396e9bdd64294), with invoice_id matching either the vendor invoice number VEN-030-736427 or the SAP record id apinv_e42396e9bdd64294, and gl_account set to 219000.
justification: scen_036 is the third CrownPeak Northstar item and rounds out the eight-invoice partner round; its stale Q1-2026 IR-retainer cancellation status must be captured in the tracker for the partner round. Either invoice_id form is a valid agent action: OE 5 prescribes the VEN-NNN form (VEN-030-736427 here) while the SAP record id (apinv_e42396e9bdd64294) is the equivalent reference, so the rubric accepts both.
evidence: Look in the trajectory for an Airtable upsert on the AP Workflow Exceptions table referencing either invoice_id VEN-030-736427 or apinv_e42396e9bdd64294, with gl_account 219000.

criterion: The Agent sends an email from ben.arinzo@brookfieldcpas.com to owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com with a subject related to the weekly AP accrual context for Daniel's partner round (or similar).
justification: The prompt explicitly directs Ben to send the consolidated table to Owen so he is looped before Daniel pulls him in. The recipient and sender are fixed; the subject can be agent-generated freetext as long as it reflects the weekly AP accrual write-up context.
evidence: Look in the trajectory for an outbound email with sender ben.arinzo@brookfieldcpas.com and recipient owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com whose subject references the weekly AP accrual or partner-round context.

criterion: The Agent's email to Owen Mercer groups the eight escalated AP items into three sub-groups by signing partner: Steven Perry covering the three Acme Cloud items (TimeLedger Nexus VEN-010-514242, GraniteRack VEN-012-753165, TimeLedger Nexus VEN-010-693199); Matthew Li covering the three Northstar Legal items (CrownPeak VEN-030-353041, CrownPeak VEN-030-817856, CrownPeak apinv_e42396e9bdd64294); and Andrea Phil covering the two firm-internal Brookfield items (LatticeHill VEN-033-86573, Pinecrest VEN-006-193120).
justification: The prompt explicitly requires the consolidated write-up to be grouped by signing partner (Steven on Acme, Matthew on Northstar, Andrea on the firm's own books). This is the structural ask for the email body.
evidence: Look in the trajectory at the email body sent to owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com and confirm the eight invoices are organized into three partner-named sub-groups matching the entity-to-partner mapping above.

criterion: The Agent's email to Owen Mercer includes, for each of the eight invoices, both the disputed invoice amount and the cumulative accrued carrying balance on the relevant payable account, together with an explicit tie or no-tie result comparing the two.
justification: The prompt directly asks Ben to surface the cumulative accrued balance against the disputed invoice amount and flag anything where the balance does not tie. The consolidated email to Owen is where that comparison must land for the partner round.
evidence: Look in the trajectory at the email body sent to owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com and confirm each of the eight invoices has both the disputed amount and the cumulative carrying balance shown, with a tie/no-tie disposition stated for each.

criterion: The Agent posts a message in the #vendor-bills-and-ap Slack channel (channel_id C010) that tags Daniel Jones (daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com).
justification: The prompt's last instruction is to post the rundown in the AP channel and tag Daniel before he sits down to write the partner brief. The channel and the tagged person are both named in the prompt, so they are fixed.
evidence: Look in the trajectory for a Slack post to the channel named "#vendor-bills-and-ap" or its id C010 with a tag or mention referring to Daniel Jones.

criterion: The Agent's Slack post in #vendor-bills-and-ap explicitly flags three invoices as do-not-release for this partner round: scen_031 GraniteRack Compute Services (VEN-012-753165 / Acme) on a superseded SOW; scen_033 CrownPeak Cyber Defense (VEN-030-353041 / Northstar) as a duplicate tied to the vendor's February 2026 platform migration; and scen_036 CrownPeak Cyber Defense (apinv_e42396e9bdd64294 / Northstar) as a stale Q1-2026 IR-retainer cancellation that was never voided in the AP record.
justification: The prompt asks Ben to make sure anything that should not be released because of a contract or cancellation question is surfaced so nobody pushes it through. The Slack rundown is the channel-visible signal of those three items.
evidence: Look in the trajectory at the Slack post body and confirm each of the three do-not-release invoices (scen_031 GraniteRack, scen_033 CrownPeak duplicate, scen_036 CrownPeak stale cancellation) is named with its do-not-release reason.

criterion: The Agent adds a comment on a Linear issue in the AP workflow standardization project (project id linear_63697b7dff5c) that references the scen_031 GraniteRack Compute Services invoice (VEN-012-753165 or apinv_6131b7c637aa4b6e) and warns the next AP batch reviewer not to release the invoice because the active SOW (SOW-2025-GR-rev1) post-dates the period the invoice was billed under.
justification: The prompt directs Ben to leave a comment on the related Linear issue for anything that should not be released, so the next batch does not push it through. scen_031 is the GraniteRack superseded-SOW item Daniel will need flagged for Steven's sign-off.
evidence: Look in the trajectory for a Linear comment under the linear_63697b7dff5c project that names the GraniteRack invoice (VEN-012-753165 or apinv_6131b7c637aa4b6e) and states a superseded-SOW or contract-scope do-not-release reason.

criterion: The Agent adds a comment on a Linear issue in the AP workflow standardization project (project id linear_63697b7dff5c) that references the scen_033 CrownPeak Cyber Defense invoice (VEN-030-353041 or apinv_e0235997b4964a8f) and warns the next AP batch reviewer not to release the invoice because it traces to a duplicate billing on the vendor's February 2026 platform migration.
justification: The prompt asks for a Linear comment on every do-not-release item. scen_033 is the duplicate CrownPeak charge tied to the vendor's platform migration and must be flagged so the next batch reviewer does not release it alongside the sibling charge that was already paid.
evidence: Look in the trajectory for a Linear comment under the linear_63697b7dff5c project that names the CrownPeak invoice VEN-030-353041 (or apinv_e0235997b4964a8f) and states a duplicate-billing do-not-release reason tied to the February 2026 CrownPeak platform migration.

criterion: The Agent adds a comment on a Linear issue in the AP workflow standardization project (project id linear_63697b7dff5c) that references the scen_036 CrownPeak Cyber Defense invoice (apinv_e42396e9bdd64294) and warns the next AP batch reviewer not to release the invoice because the underlying IR-retainer engagement was cancelled in Q1 2026 but the AP record was never voided.
justification: The prompt explicitly calls out stale engagement cancellations as one of the do-not-release patterns the partner needs to know about. scen_036 is the CrownPeak IR-retainer cancellation never voided, and the prompt requires that the Linear standardization project carry a comment warning the next batch reviewer.
evidence: Look in the trajectory for a Linear comment under the linear_63697b7dff5c project that names apinv_e42396e9bdd64294 and states a stale-cancellation or wound-down-engagement do-not-release reason.

criterion: The Agent sends the consolidated email to Owen Mercer before posting the rundown that tags Daniel Jones in #vendor-bills-and-ap.
justification: The prompt requires that Owen be looped in before Daniel pulls him in for the partner brief, with "Send the consolidated table to Owen at the same time so he's looped before Daniel pulls him in" followed by "post the rundown in the AP channel and tag Daniel." Both write actions have their own 1.1 rubrics that pass regardless of order, so no Outcome rubric can verify the ordering; this is the rare case where a Process rubric is warranted.
evidence: In the trajectory, locate the timestamp on the outbound email to owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com and the timestamp on the Slack post to #vendor-bills-and-ap that tags Daniel Jones. The email timestamp must come before the Slack-post timestamp.

criterion: The Agent's Airtable AP Workflow Exceptions row updates include, for each of the eight invoices, accrual-context notes that capture both the disputed invoice amount and the cumulative accrued carrying balance on the relevant payable account, together with an explicit tie or no-tie result comparing the two (or similar).
justification: The prompt explicitly directs Ben to 'add the accrual context onto each item in the AP exceptions tracker (create the row if it isn't already there, update it if it is) so the same stale notes don't carry into next week's standup.' The required notes content is the carrying-balance-versus-disputed-amount comparison the prompt asks Ben to surface. R1–R8 verify that each of the eight rows exists with the correct invoice_id and gl_account but do not verify any content in the notes field; this rubric closes that 1.2 content gap so an agent cannot pass the Airtable upsert checks by creating skeleton rows that fail to overwrite the stale notes.
evidence: Look in the trajectory at the Airtable upserts on the AP Workflow Exceptions table (base_id airtable_e86eaf439d1d, table_id airtable_8ee8b6013413) and confirm each of the eight rows' notes field reflects the carrying-balance-vs-disputed-amount comparison together with a tie or no-tie indication.
